topic.description: The topic encompasses strategies and practices aimed at creating environmentally friendly and socially equitable urban spaces.

doc2: integration of green spaces in city design

topic.name: Naval Warfare Vessels of WWII
topic.description: The documents discuss various naval vessels used during World War II, including cruisers, battleships, and aircraft carriers from different navies.

1: The Scharnhorst class was the last traditional class of armored cruisers built by the Kaiserliche Marine.
Both ships had short careers; shortly before the outbreak of World War I, the ships departed the German colony at Tsingtao.
2: HMS Glowworm was a G-class destroyer built for the Royal Navy in the mid-1930s.
Glowworm was heavily damaged by Admiral Hipper, but still attempted to torpedo the German ship.
3: The Japanese aircraft carrier Unryū was the lead ship of her class of fleet aircraft carriers built for the Imperial Japanese Navy during World War II.
She was torpedoed and sunk by the American submarine USS Redfish in the East China Sea during the voyage.
4: The Russian battleship Sinop was a battleship built for the Imperial Russian Navy, being the third ship of the Ekaterina II class.
She was converted to a gunnery training ship in 1910 before she became a guardship at Sevastopol.
5: Admiral Scheer was a Deutschland-class heavy cruiser which served with the Kriegsmarine of Nazi Germany during World War II.
Her first operation during World War II was a commerce raiding operation into the southern Atlantic.
6: The Zuihō class was a group of two aircraft carriers built for the Imperial Japanese Navy before World War II.
Shōhō was the first Japanese aircraft carrier to be sunk during World War II.
7: Zuihō participated in the Philippine Sea and Leyte Gulf battles in mid-1944.
She was finally sunk by American aircraft fulfilling her task.
8: Mikasa is a pre-dreadnought battleship built for the Imperial Japanese Navy in the late 1890s.
She served as the flagship of Admiral Tōgō Heihachirō throughout the Russo-Japanese War of 1904 – 1905.
9: Libia was a protected cruiser built in Italy in the 1900s.
She had a relatively uneventful career.
10: Provence was a battleship of the French Navy built in the 1910s, named in honor of the French region of Provence.
She was modernized significantly in the 1920s and 1930s.

topic.name: U.S. State Highways and Routes
topic.description: The documents discuss various state highways and routes across different U.S. states, detailing their history, route descriptions, and geographical significance.

Document 1: Interstate 29 in Iowa is a north-south Interstate Highway which closely parallels the Missouri River.
I-29 was built in sections over a period of 15 years.
Document 2: Delaware Route 15 is a state highway in the U.S. state of Delaware.
DE 15 winds a path through many rural sections of Delaware.
Document 3: Delaware Route 58 is a state highway in New Castle County, Delaware.
DE 58 was designated onto it in the 1980s.
Document 4: M-147 was a state trunkline highway in the U.S. state of Michigan.
The route of M-147 was transferred to local control in 1991.
Document 6: M-68 is an east-west state trunkline highway located in the northern part of the Lower Peninsula of Michigan.
The current designation was created in 1936.
Document 7: State Route 370 is a 1.21-mile long north-south state highway in Ohio.
SR 370 was established in the middle of the 1930s.
Document 9: State Route 92 is a state highway in the U.S. state of Arizona.
The route was constructed and numbered as SR 92 in the late 1930s.
Document 10: U.S. Route 197 is a north-south United States Highway, of which all but 2.76 miles are within Oregon.
US 197 was established in 1952 using the existing The Dalles-California Highway.

topic.name: Popular Music Releases
topic.description: The documents discuss various popular music releases, including albums and singles by notable artists across different genres and eras.

1: 5to Piso is the eleventh studio album by Guatemalan singer-songwriter Ricardo Arjona, released on 18 November 2008.
Commercially and critically successful, 5to Piso became Arjona's second number-one set on the Billboard Top Latin Albums.
2: 'Never Let You Go' is a song performed by Canadian recording artist Justin Bieber, released as a promo single from his debut album, My World 2.0 on March 2, 2010.
The song charted within the top twenty in Canada and New Zealand, twenty-one in the United States.
3: 'My Heart Is Calling' is a song recorded by American recording artist Whitney Houston for the 1996 film The Preacher's Wife.
The song was released on June 10, 1997 as the third and final single from the accompanying soundtrack.
4: 'My Moment' is a song by American recording artist Rebecca Black, released on July 19, 2011.
The song is a self-empowerment follow-up song to her first single 'Friday'.
5: 'Pero Me Acuerdo de Ti' is a song written and produced by Rudy Pérez, first recorded by Puerto Rican singer Lourdes Robles.
American recording artist Christina Aguilera performed a cover version on her second studio album Mi Reflejo.
6: 'Feeling This' is a song recorded by American rock band Blink-182 for their eponymously titled fifth studio album.
The song was released as the lead single from Blink-182 on October 2, 2003.
7: 'Just a Fool' is a duet recorded by American singer songwriters Christina Aguilera and Blake Shelton for Aguilera's seventh studio album, Lotus.
It was sent to contemporary hit and hot adult contemporary radio stations in the United States.
8: 'Europa and the Pirate Twins' is a song by English artist Thomas Dolby, featured on his 1982 album The Golden Age of Wireless.
The song was released as a single on 3 October 1981.
9: Circus is the sixth studio album by American recording artist Britney Spears, released on November 28, 2008.
The album topped charts in nine additional countries.
10: 'No Can Do' is a song by English girl group Sugababes from their sixth studio album, Catfights and Spotlights (2008).
The song was released in the United Kingdom and Ireland on 19 December 2008.

topic.name: Cricket and Professional Wrestling
topic.description: This cluster discusses notable figures and events in the sports of cricket and professional wrestling.

1: Bob Willis is an English former cricketer who played for Surrey, Warwickshire, Northern Transvaal and England.
Willis spearheaded several England bowling attacks between 1971 and 1984, across 90 Test matches.
2: Leah Poulton is a female Australian cricketer who plays for New South Wales and Australia.
Poulton came to prominence in youth cricket by captaining New South Wales to the Under-17 national championships in 2000.
3: Gail Kim-Irvine is a Canadian professional wrestler, professional wrestling valet, model and actress.
She is also known for her time with World Wrestling Entertainment (WWE).
4: Cyber Sunday (2007) was the fourth annual Cyber Sunday professional wrestling pay-per-view event produced by World Wrestling Entertainment (WWE).
The most important feature of Cyber Sunday is the ability for fans to vote online through WWE.com on certain aspects of every match.
5: The CMLL World Middleweight Championship is a professional wrestling world championship promoted by the Mexican wrestling promotion Consejo Mundial de Lucha Libre (CMLL).
The title is awarded to a wrestler after he 'wins' a match to maintain the illusion that professional wrestling is a competitive sport.
6: Ashley Marie Massaro is an American former professional wrestler, valet, former WWE Diva and current general manager signed to World Independent Ladies Division Wrestling.
She is best known for her time with World Wrestling Entertainment (WWE) under the ring name Ashley.
7: Survivor Series (1994) was the eighth annual Survivor Series pay-per-view professional wrestling event produced by the World Wrestling Federation (WWF).
Two main event matches were held; in the first, Bob Backlund won the WWF World Heavyweight Championship from Bret Hart.
8: Murali Kartik is an Indian cricketer who sporadically represented the India national cricket team from 2000 to 2007.
He is a specialist slow left arm orthodox bowler known for his loopy trajectory and ability to spin and bounce.
9: On 11 and 12 February 1851, teams from Port Phillip, Victoria and Van Diemen's Land played a timeless cricket match at the Launceston Racecourse.
The match was held under the aegis of the Launceston Cricket Club.
10: Joseph Charles 'Jos' Buttler is an English cricketer who plays for Lancashire County Cricket Club and England.
He is a right-handed batsman who also plays as a wicket-keeper and is the current Vice-Captain of the England ODI team.

topic.name: Tropical Cyclones and Hurricanes
topic.description: The documents discuss the formation, impact, and meteorological history of various tropical cyclones and hurricanes that occurred in different years and regions.

1: Tropical Storm Chris caused minor flooding along the Gulf Coast of the United States in September 1982.
The storm caused steady rainfall in parts of the Midwestern United States.
3: Tropical Storm Gabrielle caused moderate flooding in northeastern Mexico and southern Texas in August 1995.
The storm produced torrential rainfall in northeastern Mexico, totaling to 19.44 inches (494 mm).
4: Hurricane Eugene was the only tropical cyclone to make landfall in Mexico during the 1987 Pacific hurricane season.
The hurricane deluged the southwest Mexican coastline, resulting in the highest rainfall totals from a tropical cyclone in five Mexican states.
5: Hurricane Paul was a particularly deadly and destructive Pacific hurricane which killed a total of 1,696 people and caused $ 1.156 billion in damage.
The storm changed little in strength for several days until September 25, when it slowly intensified into a tropical storm.
6: The Effects of Hurricane Isaac in Florida were severe local flooding in South Florida and tornadoes in the central and northern portions of the state in August 2012.
Despite passing closest to the Florida Keys, impact was minimal, limited to tropical storm-force winds and light rainfall.
7: Hurricane Danny in August 2015 was the first major hurricane to develop between the Lesser Antilles and Western Africa since Hurricane Julia in 2010.
The hurricane prompted the issuance of several tropical storm warnings for the Lesser Antilles.
8: Subtropical Storm One was a short-lived subtropical cyclone that brought copious amounts of rainfall to central Florida in late June 1974.
The heavy rainfall led to moderate flooding across the state.
9: Hurricane Ignacio threatened Hawaii during July 1985.
Ignacio weakened quickly, though it briefly leveled off in intensity as a Category 2 hurricane.
10: Hurricane Allison was the first named storm and first hurricane of the 1995 Atlantic hurricane season.
It strengthened into a tropical storm early on June 3 and into a hurricane on June 4 in the Gulf of Mexico.

topic.name: Shark Species and Biology
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various species of sharks, their biological characteristics, habitats, and conservation status.

Document 1: The frilled shark (Chlamydoselachus anguineus) is one of two extant species of shark in the family Chlamydoselachidae
Exhibiting several 'primitive' features, the frilled shark has often been termed a 'living fossil'.
Document 2: The smalltooth sand tiger or bumpytail ragged-tooth (Odontaspis ferox) is a species of mackerel shark in the family Odontaspididae
This rare species is often mistaken for the much more common grey nurse shark (Carcharias taurus).
Document 3: The winghead shark (Eusphyra blochii) is a species of hammerhead shark, and part of the family Sphyrnidae
It gives birth to live young, with the developing embryos receiving nourishment through a placental connection.
Document 5: The bignose shark (Carcharhinus altimus) is a species of requiem shark, in the family Carcharhinidae
Hunting close to the sea floor, the bignose shark feeds on bony and cartilaginous fishes, and cephalopods.

topic.name: Medieval English Castles
topic.description: The documents discuss various medieval castles in England, detailing their historical significance, architectural features, and roles during conflicts such as the English Civil War.

Document 1: Wressle Castle is a ruined palace-fortress in the East Riding of Yorkshire, England, built for Thomas Percy in the 1390s.
Wressle Castle was mostly under royal control until 1471 when it was returned to the Percy family.
Document 2: Beeston Castle is a former Royal castle in Beeston, Cheshire, perched on a rocky sandstone crag 350 feet above the Cheshire Plain.
The castle was slighted (partly demolished) in 1646, in accordance with Cromwell's destruction order.
Document 3: St Catherine's Castle is a Henrician castle in Cornwall, built by Thomas Treffry between approximately 1538 and 1540.
The castle remained in use for many years until it was closed at the end of the Napoleonic Wars in 1815.
Document 4: Longtown Castle is a ruined Norman motte-and-bailey fortification in Longtown, Herefordshire.
Despite being pressed back into use during the Owain Glyndŵr rising in 1403, it became ruined.
Document 5: St Mawes Castle is an artillery fort constructed by Henry VIII near Falmouth, Cornwall, between 1540 and 1542.
During the English Civil War, St Mawes was held by Royalist supporters of King Charles I.
Document 6: Conisbrough Castle is a medieval fortification in Conisbrough, South Yorkshire, England.
The castle remained in the family line into the 14th century, despite being seized several times by the Crown.
Document 7: Sandsfoot Castle, also known historically as Weymouth Castle, is an artillery fort constructed by Henry VIII near Weymouth, Dorset.
Sandsfoot saw service during the English Civil War, when it was held by Parliament and Royalists in turn.
Document 8: Stokesay Castle is a fortified manor house in Stokesay, Shropshire, England.
After the Royalist war effort collapsed in 1645, Parliamentary forces besieged the castle in June and quickly forced its garrison to surrender.
Document 10: Dunstanburgh Castle is a 14th-century fortification on the coast of Northumberland in northern England.
The castle also served as a statement of the earl's wealth and influence, and would have invited comparisons with the neighbouring royal castle of Bamburgh.

explores Naju in a non-linear fashion

topic.name: American Political Figures and Events
topic.description: The documents collectively discuss notable American political figures and events spanning various historical periods and contexts.

Document 1: Jane Fonda is an American actress, writer, political activist
Document 2: General Joel Palmer was an American pioneer of the Oregon Territory
served as a member of the Indiana House of Representatives
Document 4: William Jason Fields was a politician from the U.S. state of Kentucky
he represented Kentucky's Ninth District in the U.S. House of Representatives

he represented Massachusetts in the First and Second Continental Congresses

served as the 29th Governor of New Mexico

topic.name: Notable Figures in Sports and Entertainment
topic.description: This topic encompasses significant personalities and events in various sports and entertainment fields, highlighting their achievements and contributions.

1: Laura Robson is a British tennis player and an Olympic Mixed Doubles Silver Medalist.
Robson was named WTA Newcomer of the Year for 2012.
4: Monk McDonald was an American college athlete and head coach for the North Carolina Tar Heels men's basketball team.
He is generally considered the best all-around college athlete to attend the University of North Carolina.
9: Mary Wayte is an American former competition swimmer and two-time Olympic gold medalist.
Wayte won eight medals in major international championships, including four golds.
5: The Federer – Nadal rivalry is between two professional tennis players, Roger Federer and Rafael Nadal.
They are currently engaged in a storied rivalry, which many consider to be the greatest in tennis history.

topic.name: Cultural Representations in Media
topic.description: The documents explore various cultural representations and themes in media, including television, film, music, and sports.

Document 1: Code of Honor is an episode that explores themes of honor and cultural conflict in a science fiction context.
The episode was described as having a '1940s tribal Africa' theme.
Document 3: Nolan's films are rooted in philosophical, sociological and ethical concepts, exploring human morality and identity.
His body of work is permeated by metafictive elements and nonlinear storytelling.
Document 6: From Elvis in Memphis showcases the blending of country and soul music, highlighting cultural influences in American music.
The album marked Presley's return to non-soundtrack albums, reflecting a shift in his artistic direction.
Document 7: The ACC Championship Game highlights the cultural significance of college football in American society.
Wake Forest's victory represents an underdog story in sports culture.

topic.name: Historical Innovations in Transportation
topic.description: This topic encompasses the development and impact of various transportation technologies and infrastructures, including telegraphs and roller coasters, reflecting their significance in history and modern society.

1: The Cooke and Wheatstone telegraph was an early electrical telegraph system dating from the 1830s invented by English inventor William Fothergill Cooke and English scientist Charles Wheatstone.
It was the first telegraph system to be put into commercial service.
2: Superman: Krypton Coaster is a Bolliger & Mabillard Floorless Coaster at the Six Flags Fiesta Texas amusement park.
It is also known for being the world's tallest and fastest floorless coaster at 168 ft (51 m) high and a top speed of 70 mph (110 km/h).
3: Leviathan is a steel roller coaster in the Medieval Faire section of the park at Canada's Wonderland in Vaughan, Ontario, Canada.
It is the first roller coaster made by Swiss manufacturer Bolliger & Mabillard to exceed a height of 300 feet (91 m).
5: Iron Rattler is a steel roller coaster located at Six Flags Fiesta Texas in San Antonio, Texas.
It is the first hybrid wood and steel roller coaster to feature an inversion.

topic.name: Automotive and Racing Events
topic.description: This topic encompasses various aspects of automotive culture, including notable car models, racing events, and significant figures in the motorsport industry.

1: Iron Man 2 is a 2010 American superhero film based on the Marvel Comics character Iron Man, produced by Marvel Studios and distributed by Paramount Pictures.
It is the sequel to 2008's Iron Man, and is the third film in the Marvel Cinematic Universe.
2: The 2009 AAA 400 was the twenty-eighth stock car race of the 2009 NASCAR Sprint Cup Series.
The race was Johnson's fourth win of the 2009 season, as well as the forty-fourth of his career.
3: The Jaguar XJ220 is a two-seater supercar produced by British luxury car manufacturer Jaguar from 1992 until 1994.
Recording a top speed of 212.3 mph (342 km/h) in 1992 the XJ220 could keep up with the fastest production car since 1987.
4: The 2000 Spanish Grand Prix was a Formula One motor race held on 7 May 2000 at the Circuit de Catalunya.
It was the fifth round of the 2000 Formula One season and the 42nd Spanish Grand Prix.
5: The 2007 Toyota / Save Mart 350 was the sixteenth stock car race of the 2007 NASCAR Nextel Cup Series.
It was held on June 24, 2007 at Infineon Raceway in Sonoma, California.
6: Wilhelm Steinitz was an Austrian and later American chess Master player, and the first undisputed world chess champion from 1886 to 1894.
He was also a highly influential writer and chess theoretician.
7: The 2009 Dickies 500 was the 34th stock car race of the 2009 NASCAR Sprint Cup Series.
The race was Kurt Busch's second win of the 2009 season, his first at the Texas Motor Speedway.
8: The 2009 Samsung 500 was the seventh stock car race of the 2009 NASCAR Sprint Cup Series.
It was held on April 5, 2009 at Texas Motor Speedway in Fort Worth, Texas before a crowd of 176,300 people.
9: David B. Falk is an American sports agent who primarily works with basketball players in the National Basketball Association.
Falk began his career representing professional tennis players for Donald Dell's ProServ.
10: The 2007 French Grand Prix was a Formula One motor race held on 1 July 2007 at the Circuit de Nevers Magny-Cours.
It was the eighth race of the 2007 Formula One season.

topic.name: Animal Breeds and Species
topic.description: The documents discuss various breeds of animals, including dogs and species of sharks and birds, highlighting their characteristics, history, and ecological significance.

10: The Welsh Springer Spaniel is a breed of dog and a member of the spaniel family.
They were bred for hunting and are known for their loyalty and affection.

topic.name: Culinary Traditions
topic.description: The documents explore various culinary traditions and dishes from different cultures, highlighting their historical significance and preparation methods.

1: A pasty is a baked pastry, a traditional variety of which is particularly associated with Cornwall, in the United Kingdom.
The traditional Cornish pasty...is filled with beef, sliced or diced potato, swede...and onion.
2: Fried chicken is a dish consisting of chicken pieces usually from broiler chickens which have been floured or battered and then pan-fried, deep fried, or pressure fried.
The dish was first brought to the United States by Scottish immigrants.
3: Palestinian cuisine consists of foods from or commonly eaten by Palestinians.
It is similar to other Levantine cuisines, including Lebanese, Syrian and Jordanian.
4: Mediterranean cuisine is the food from the lands around the Mediterranean Sea and its preparation.
The region's food came to be seen as a more or less unified cuisine.

topic.name: History of Comics and Animation
topic.description: This topic encompasses the development, styles, and influential figures in the fields of comics and animation, particularly in North America.

1: Ralph Bakshi is an American director of animated and live-action films.
He established an alternative to mainstream animation through independent and adult-oriented productions.
2: Jimmy Frise is best known for his work on the comic strip Birdseye Center.
Frise continued cartooning at the Star upon his return from World War I.
3: Canadian comics refers to comics and cartooning by citizens of Canada or permanent residents of Canada.
Canada has two official languages, and distinct comics cultures have developed in English and French Canada.
4: Comics is a medium used to express ideas by images, often combined with text or other visual information.
Common forms of comics include comic strips, editorial and gag cartoons, and comic books.
5: Avatar: The Last Airbender is an American animated television series that aired for three seasons on Nickelodeon.
The show combines the styles of anime and American cartoons.
6: Little Annie Fanny is a comics series by Harvey Kurtzman and Will Elder.
The series is notable for its painted, luminous color artwork and for being the first full-scale, multi-page comics feature in a major American publication.
9: It's a Good Life, If You Don't Weaken is a graphic novel by Canadian cartoonist Seth.
The story unfolds with a nostalgic and melancholic tone.
10: LGBT themes in American mainstream comics is a relatively new concept.
Historically, LGBT themes and characters were omitted intentionally from the content of comic books.

topic.name: Mathematics and Electrical Engineering
topic.description: The documents discuss various mathematical concepts and their applications in electrical engineering and physics.

1: negative resistance is a property of some electrical circuits
used in electronic oscillators and amplifiers
2: a matrix is a rectangular array of numbers
major application of matrices is to represent linear transformations
3: a Taylor series is a representation of a function as an infinite sum
Taylor's theorem gives quantitative estimates on the error
4: Foster's reactance theorem is important in electrical network analysis
reactance of a passive, lossless two-terminal network increases with frequency
5: the weak interaction is one of the four fundamental interactions of nature
responsible for radioactive decay
6: the derivative measures the sensitivity to change of a quantity
derivatives are a fundamental tool of calculus
7: the binary logarithm is the power to which the number 2 must be raised
used to calculate the length of the representation of a number
8: the Dirac delta function is a generalized function on the real number line
physically represents the density of an idealized point mass
9: Hilbert space generalizes the notion of Euclidean space
indispensable tools in the theories of partial differential equations and quantum mechanics
10: roots of the reconstructed Proto-Indo-European language carry a lexical meaning
complete inflected words are formed by adding further morphemes to a root

topic.name: Sacred Music Compositions
topic.description: The documents discuss various sacred music compositions by notable composers, focusing on their historical context, structure, and themes related to religious liturgy.

1: Gabriel Fauré composed his Requiem in D minor, Op. 48, between 1887 and 1890.
Its focus is on eternal rest and consolation.
2: Die Himmel erzählen die Ehre Gottes (The heavens are telling the glory of God), BWV 76, is a cantata by Johann Sebastian Bach.
Bach composed the cantata at a decisive turning point in his career.
5: Aus der Tiefen rufe ich, Herr, zu dir (Out of the depths I call, Lord, to You), BWV 131, is a church cantata by Johann Sebastian Bach.
The text is based on Luther's German version of Psalm 130.

topic.name: Legal and Political Frameworks
topic.description: This cluster discusses various aspects of legal and political systems, including constitutional provisions, political scandals, and judicial structures.

4: The Oregon Supreme Court (OSC) is the highest state court in the U.S. state of Oregon.
the court has grown from a single judge to its current make up of seven justices.
7: The United States Constitution contains several provisions regarding the law of criminal procedure.
Several of these rights regulate pre-trial procedure.

topic.name: Religious Institutions and Movements
topic.description: The documents discuss various religious institutions and movements, their histories, and their cultural significance.

1: Congregation Beth Israel is a Jewish congregation located in Scottsdale, Arizona.
It affiliated with Reform Judaism in 1935.
2: Sängerfest is a competition of Sängerbunds, or singer groups, associated with Germanic culture.
It was adapted by Christian churches for spiritual worship services.
3: Antoinism is a healer and Christian-oriented new religious movement founded in 1910.
The movement is characterized by a decentralized structure and tolerance towards other faiths.
4: Robert Jefferson Breckinridge was a politician and Presbyterian minister.
He became an ordained minister in 1832.
5: St Fflewin's Church is a rural church situated in Anglesey, north Wales.
The church is still used for worship by the Church in Wales.
7: Unitarian Universalist Church of Arlington has taken part in progressive causes from the Civil Rights Movement to the legalization of same-sex marriage.
It was the first Unitarian church in Washington, D.C.'s suburbs.
8: Christ Episcopal Church is a historic Episcopal church in Waltham, Massachusetts.
The church was founded in 1848 and is a parish of the Episcopal Diocese of Massachusetts.
9: The Catholic Church is the largest Christian church, with more than 1.27 billion members.
It has played a prominent role in the history of Western civilization.

topic.name: Diverse Business Ventures
topic.description: The documents discuss various businesses and organizations across different sectors, including technology, sports, finance, media, and entertainment.

1: Hightail is a cloud service that lets users send, receive, digitally sign and synchronize files.
The company grew quickly and raised $49 million in funding.
2: Maple Leaf Sports & Entertainment is a professional sports and commercial real estate company based in Toronto, Ontario, Canada.
It is the largest sports and entertainment company in Canada.
3: Norman Hsu is a convicted pyramid investment promoter who associated himself with the apparel industry.
He gained notoriety after suspicious patterns of bundled campaign contributions were reported in 2007.
4: NuScale Power designs and markets small modular reactors (SMRs).
The company was founded based on research funded by the Department of Energy.
5: Billboard publishes news, video, opinion, reviews, events and style related to the entertainment industry.
It is known for music charts, including the Billboard Hot 100 and Billboard 200.
6: The Potato Sack is an alternate reality game created by the Valve Corporation to promote the release of Portal 2.
It involved players solving puzzles to release the game early.
7: Shriya Saran is an Indian film actress and model who has acted in multiple language films.
She gained commercial success with her role in the 2007 film Sivaji.
8: Emilie de Ravin is an Australian actress known for her roles in Roswell and Lost.
She starred in several films and was included on Maxim's Hot 100 list.
9: Tory Burch is an American fashion designer and businesswoman who has won several fashion awards.
She was listed as the 73rd most powerful woman in the world by Forbes.
10: The Grameen Bank is a microfinance organization that makes small loans to the impoverished without requiring collateral.
It was founded by Muhammad Yunus and jointly awarded the Nobel Peace Prize in 2006.
